key_insight: The paper shows that autonomous LLM research can be made reliable in frontier physics by grounding every stage in literature, reproduction, and adversarial review rather than relying on internal priors.
engineering_relevance: It proposes a fault-tolerant multi-session pipeline that can move from literature mining to validated first-principles computation and manuscript drafting, suggesting a template for high-stakes scientific automation.
method: Mapped 11,083 recent condensed-matter arXiv papers to identify a research direction.
Used fresh-context sessions with on-disk shared state, plus literature reproduction checkpoints to calibrate methodology.
Ran novel first-principles computations and adversarial reviews before writing the manuscript.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 47 fresh-context sessions, 2,162 literature-consultation events, and three substantive physics findings, with paired ablations used to isolate the role of calibration checkpoints.
watch_item: The approach still depends on reproduction access, curated literature anchors, and bounded human intervention when calibration failures occur, so robustness may vary across domains.
paper_title: Grounded autonomous research: a fault-tolerant LLM pipeline from corpus to manuscript in frontier computational physics
